The number of calibres is determined by dividing the length of the bore of the weapon, from the breech face of the tube to the muzzle, by the diameter of its bore. A gun tube the bore of which is 40 feet (480 inches) long and 12 inches in diameter is said to be 40 calibers long.

(intransitive, as the phrasal verb commiserate with) To sympathize; condole.
(business) Anything movable (a good) that is bought and sold. [from 15th c.]
Something useful or valuable. [from 15th c.]
(economics) Raw materials, agricultural and other primary products as objects of large-scale trading in specialized exchanges.
(usually singular in construction) A common#Noun (common land); especially, a central section of (usually an older) town, designated as a shared area.
(figuratively) The mutual good of all; the abstract concept of resources shared by more than one, for example air, water, information.
(obsolete) The well-being of a community.
The entirety of a (secular) society, a polity, a state.
The act or fact of communicating anything; transmission.

examples.english: English is a means of communication.

current_entries.en_definition: The concept or state of exchanging data or information between entities.
A message; the essential data transferred in an act of communication.
A passageway or opening between two locations; connection.
(anatomy) A connection between two tissues, organs, or cavities.
Any far-left political ideology or philosophy advocating holding the production of resources collectively, especially by seizing it through revolution.

examples.english: Communism will never be reached in my lifetime.

current_entries.en_definition: The international socialist society where classes, money, and the state no longer exist.
A group sharing common characteristics, such as the same language, law, religion, or tradition.

examples.english: The community is made up of individuals.

current_entries.en_definition: A residential or religious collective; a commune.
(countable, ecology) A group of interdependent organisms inhabiting the same region and interacting with each other.
The condition of having certain attitudes and interests in common.
The act of comparing or the state or process of being compared.

examples.english: The comparison between the two was to his disadvantage.

current_entries.en_definition: An evaluation of the similarities and differences of one or more things relative to some other or each other.
A room, or section, or chamber, typically within a vehicle.

examples.english: I found the secret compartment quite by accident.

current_entries.en_definition: One of the parts into which an area is subdivided.
To pay or reward someone in exchange for work done or some other consideration.

examples.english: You must compensate him for the money he lost.

current_entries.en_definition: (ambitransitive) To make up for; to do something in place of something else; to correct, satisfy; to reach an agreement such that the scales are literally or (metaphorically) balanced; to equalize or make even.
The act or principle of compensating.
Something which is regarded as an equivalent; something which compensates for loss.
(finance) The extinction of debts of which two persons are reciprocally debtors by the credits of which they are reciprocally creditors; the payment of a debt by a credit of equal amount.

(real estate) An equivalent stipulated for in contracts for the sale of real estate, in which it is customary to provide that errors in description, etc., shall not avoid, but shall be the subject of compensation.
To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend.
To take part in a contest, game or similar event.
(intransitive) To express feelings of pain, dissatisfaction, or resentment.

examples.english: How dare he complain?

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To make a formal accusation or bring a formal charge.
One who makes complaint, one who complains.
(legal) The party that brings a civil lawsuit against another; the plaintiff.
In a complete manner; thoroughly.

examples.english: After running up the hill, I was completely out of breath.

current_entries.en_definition: To the fullest extent or degree; totally.
The state of being complex; intricacy; entanglement.

examples.english: He's contemplating the complexity of contemporary life.

current_entries.en_definition: That which is and renders complex; intricacy; complication.
Especially, one who composes music.
One who composes; an author.
(transitive) To understand or grasp fully and thoroughly; to plumb. [from 14th c.]

examples.english: The professor was unable to comprehend what I meant.

current_entries.en_definition: (now, _, rare) To include, comprise; to contain. [from 14th c.]
(transitive) To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts). [from the earlier 15th c.]
(patent law) To include, contain, or be made up of, defining the minimum elements, whether essential or inessential to define an invention.
A companion in battle; fellow soldier.
A mate, companion, or associate. [from 1590s]
(communism, by extension) A fellow socialist, communist or other similarly politically aligned person. [from 1884]
(communism) A non-hierarchical title, functionally similar to "Mr.", "Mrs.", "Miss", "Ms." etc, in a communist, socialist, or rarely in an Islamist state. [from 1884]
(ambitransitive) To have a child; to become pregnant (with).

examples.english: I can't conceive of living without him.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To develop; to form in the mind; to imagine.
(transitive) To understand (someone).
The act or process of removing the dress of ore and of reducing the valuable part to smaller compass, as by currents of air or water.
The act, process or ability of concentrating; the process of becoming concentrated, or the state of being concentrated.
(physical chemistry) The amount of solute in a solution measured in suitable units (e.g., parts per million (ppm))
The proportion of a substance in a whole.
That which affects oneβÄôs welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone.

examples.english: I appreciate your concern.

current_entries.en_definition: The expression of solicitude, anxiety, or compassion toward a thing or person.
(transitive) To relate or belong to; to have reference to or connection with; to affect the interest of; to be of importance to.
A closed assembly at which cardinals elect a pope.
A group of cardinals assembled to elect a new pope.
The set of apartments in which cardinals are secluded while the process to elect a pope takes place.
The end, finish, close or last part of something.

examples.english: We've come to the conclusion that this is a true story.

current_entries.en_definition: The outcome or result of a process or act.
A decision reached after careful thought.
(logic) In an argument or syllogism, the proposition that follows as a necessary consequence of the premises.
arrangement; settlement.
(transitive) To strongly criticise or denounce; to excoriate.
(transitive) To confer eternal divine punishment upon.
(transitive) To adjudge (a building) as being unfit for habitation.
The act or process of condensing or of being condensed
(physics, physical chemistry) The conversion of a gas to a liquid.
(chemistry) The reaction of two substances with the simultaneous loss of water or other small molecule.
Limited by a condition.
(logic) Stating that one sentence is true if another is true.
(grammar) The conditional mood.

A flexible sleeve made of latex or other impermeable material such as polyurethane or sheepskin, worn over an erect penis during sexual intercourse as a contraceptive or as a way to prevent the spread of STIs during PIV, anal, or oral.

examples.english: Do you have a condom?

current_entries.en_definition: (broadly) Any of various forms of flexible sleeve or barrier, variously shaped for either penetrative or receptive body parts, made of latex or other impermeable material such as polyurethane or sheepskin, worn (by anyone with ) during sexual activity to prevent pregnancy or the spread of STIs.
(transitive) To forgive, excuse or overlook (something that is considered morally wrong, offensive, or generally disliked).

examples.english: The Catholic Church doesn't condone the use of condoms.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive, legal) To forgive (marital infidelity or other marital offense).
(transitive) To allow, accept or permit (something that is considered morally wrong, offensive, or generally disliked).
Either of two New World vultures, Vultur gryphus of the Andes or Gymnogyps californianus, a nearly extinct vulture of the mountains of California.
(golf) The completion of a hole four strokes under par (a quadruple birdie, triple eagle, or double albatross).
The ability of a material to conduct electricity, heat, fluid or sound
(physics) The reciprocal of resistivity
(business) An event organized by a for-profit or non-profit organization to discuss a pressing issue, such as a new product, market trend or government regulation, with a range of speakers.

examples.english: The conference is to be held in Tokyo.

current_entries.en_definition: (Philippines, sports) A constituent tournament of a sports league in a given season.
Self-assurance.

examples.english: John has confidence in his ability.

current_entries.en_definition: A feeling of certainty; firm trust or belief; faith.
Information held in secret; a piece of information shared but to thence be kept in secret.
The coming together of things; union.
(biology) The temporary fusion of organisms, especially as part of sexual reproduction.
The act of connecting.
A feeling of understanding and ease of communication between two or more people.
An established communications or transportation link.
Alert, awake; with one's mental faculties active.

examples.english: The warrior is conscious of both his strength and his weakness.

current_entries.en_definition: Aware of one's own existence; aware of one's own awareness.
An effect; something that follows a cause as a result.
(especially when preceded by "of") Importance, influence, or significance.
An association or combination of businesses, financial institutions, or investors, for the purpose of engaging in a joint venture.
A similar arrangement among non-commercial institutions or organizations.
An association or society.

(astronomy) An arbitrary formation of stars perceived as a figure (especially one from mythology) or pattern, or a division of the sky including it, especially one officially recognized by astronomers; an asterism.
(modern, _, astronomy) Any of the 88 regions of the sky officially recognized by the International Astronomical Union, including all stars and celestial bodies in the region. [from 1920s]
A wide, seemingly unlimited, assortment.
A state of the bowels in which the evacuations are infrequent and difficult, or the intestines become filled with hardened faeces.
The act of crowding anything into a lesser space, or the state of being crowded or pressed together; condensation.
(government) The formal or informal system of primary principles and laws that regulates a government or other institutions.
(law) A legal document describing such a formal system.
The process of constructing.
Anything that has been constructed.
The trade of building structures.
(grammar) A group of words arranged to form a meaningful phrase.
The term of office of a consul.

current_entries.en_definition: The business office of a consul; a minor embassy.
A person or party that is consulted.
A person whose occupation is to be consulted for their expertise, advice, service or help in an area or specialty; a party whose business is to be similarly consulted.
Complete in every detail, perfect, absolute.

examples.english: Tom is a consummate professional.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To bring (a task, project, goal etc.) to completion; to accomplish.
(transitive) To make (a marriage) complete by engaging in first sexual intercourse.
The act of touching physically; being in close association.

examples.english: We are sorry, the person you are trying to contact is not available.

current_entries.en_definition: The establishment of communication (with).
(informal) A contact lens.
(transitive) To touch; to come into physical contact with.

The spread or transmission of such a disease.
(figuratively, by extension) The spread of anything likened to a contagious disease.

(finance) The spread of (initially small) shocks, which initially affect only a few financial institutions or a particular region of an economy, to other financial sectors and other countries whose economies were previously healthy.
(transitive) To hold inside.

examples.english: In the English language many words contain letters which are not pronounced.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To include as a part.
(transitive) To put constraints upon; to restrain; to confine; to keep within bounds.
From the same time period, coexistent in time; contemporaneous.
Modern, of the present age (shorthand for βÄ‰contemporary with the presentβÄô).
Someone or something belonging to the same time period (as someone or something else)
Without stopping; without a break, cessation, or interruption.
(mathematics, more generally, of a function between two topological spaces) Such that each open set in the target space has an open preimage (in the domain space, with respect to the given function).
An agreement between two or more parties, to perform a specific job or work order, often temporary or of fixed duration and usually governed by a written agreement.

(legal) An agreement which the law will enforce in some way. A legally binding contract must contain at least one promise, i.e., a commitment or offer, by an offeror to and accepted by an offeree to do something in the future. A contract is thus executory rather than executed.
(transitive) To gain or acquire (an illness).
The act of contradicting.
A statement that contradicts itself, i.e., a statement that claims that the same thing is true and that it is false at the same time and in the same senses of the terms.
(logic, countable) A proposition that is false for all values of its propositional variables or Boolean atoms.
Something given or offered that adds to a larger whole.
An amount of money given toward something.
The act of contributing.
(medicine, countable) Synonym of bruise.
(medicine, uncountable) Synonym of bruising.
(now, _, especially) Synonym of nunnery, a female religious community and its residence.
The buildings and pertaining surroundings in which such a community lives.
The process of preparing food by using heat.

examples.english: I'll miss your cooking.

current_entries.en_definition: The style or genre of food preparation.
(number theory, of two or more positive integers) Having no positive integer factors in common, aside from 1.
(number theory, of a positive integer) Having no positive integer factors, aside from 1, in common with one or more specified other positive integers.

(linguistics, grammar) A word, usually a verb, used to link the subject of a sentence with a predicate (usually a subject complement or an adverbial), that unites or associates the subject with the predicate.
(statistics) A function that represents the association between two or more variables, independent of the individual marginal distributions of the variables.
Having the orange-pink color of coral.

examples.english: The coral reef is the region's prime attraction.

current_entries.en_definition: Any of many species of marine invertebrates in the class Anthozoa, most of which build hard calcium carbonate skeletons and form colonies, or a colony belonging to one of those species.
A hard substance made of the skeletons of these organisms.
An island of Greece, one of the Ionian Islands. The ancient name is Corcyra (Έöœ¨œ¹ΈΚαΩΓœ¹Έ±, more often ΈöΈ≠œ¹ΈΚαΩΓœ¹Έ±), in Modern Greek KΟ©rkira (ΈöΈ≠œ¹ΈΚœÖœ¹Έ±).
The capital of Corfu.
A stone forming the base at the corner of a building.
Such a stone used ceremonially, often inscribed with the architect's and owner's names, dates and other details.
(figuratively) That which is prominent, fundamental, noteworthy, or central.

The country of the Cornish, currently having the status of a royal duchy in England belonging to the male heir apparent to the reigning monarch, and consisting at least of the Cornwall County and the Isles of Scilly.
The westernmost of the three historical counties of Jamaica, bordered by the county of Middlesex in the east; named for the English county.
The circumference of the base of the glans penis in human males.
The luminous plasma atmosphere of the Sun (the solar corona) or other star, extending millions of kilometres into space, most easily seen during a total solar eclipse.
A reciprocal, parallel or complementary relationship between two or more comparable objects.

examples.english: Correlation does not imply causation.

current_entries.en_definition: (statistics) One of the several measures of the linear statistical relationship between two random variables, indicating both the strength and direction of the relationship.
A narrow hall or passage with rooms leading off it, as in a building or in a railway carriage.
A restricted tract of land that allows passage between two places.
Airspace restricted for the passage of aircraft.
The act of corroding or the condition so produced.
(physical chemistry) Erosion by chemical action, especially oxidation.
(by extension) The gradual destruction or undermining of something.
Unpaid labor required by a feudal lord.
Labor, especially for roads or dams, in lieu of taxes.
A city and province of Calabria, Italy.
A habitational surname from Italian.
The study of the physical universe, its structure, dynamics, origin and evolution, and fate.
A metaphysical study into the origin and nature of the universe.
The universe regarded as a system with harmony and order.
Any of various mostly Mexican herbs of the genus Cosmos having radiate heads of variously coloured flowers and pinnate leaves.
A wild feline, of species Puma concolor, native to the Americas.
(North America, slang, derogatory) An older woman who actively seeks the casual, often sexual, companionship of younger men, by implication a female βÄ€sexual predatorβÄù.
A sudden, often involuntary expulsion of air from the lungs through the glottis (causing a short, explosive sound), and out through the mouth.

examples.english: Do you have any cough medicine?

current_entries.en_definition: A bout of repeated coughing (verb sense 2.1); also, a medical condition that causes one to cough.
Sometimes followed by up: to force (something) out of the lungs or throat by pushing air from the lungs through the glottis (causing a short, explosive sound), and out through the mouth.
To expel from the lungs, throat, stomach, etc. by coughing.
(of money) To pay or disburse reluctantly (perhaps influenced by coffers of money).
Somebody from a certain country.
Somebody from one's own country; a fellow countryman.
The quality of being confident, not afraid or easily intimidated, but without being incautious or inconsiderate.

examples.english: You must build up your courage.

current_entries.en_definition: The ability to overcome one's fear, do or live things which one finds frightening.
Polite behavior.
A polite gesture or remark, especially as opposed to an obligation or standard practice.
Consent or agreement in spite of fact; indulgence.
An amount by which something or someone is covered.
(genetics) The average number of reads representing a given nucleotide in the reconstructed sequence.
A man who tends free-range cattle, especially in the American West.
A man who identifies with cowboy culture, including wearing a cowboy hat and being a fan of country and western music.
(constellation) A dim spring constellation of the northern sky, said to resemble a cup. It lies between the constellations Virgo and Hydra.

examples.english: Some of the bluest water in the world is found in Crater Lake.

current_entries.en_definition: (astronomy) A hemispherical pit created by the impact of a meteorite or other object. [from 1831]

The pit left by the explosion of a mine or bomb. [from 1839]
(ambitransitive) To desire strongly, so as to satisfy an appetite; to long or yearn for.

examples.english: The heat is making me crave for an ice cream.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive, archaic, or, literary) To ask for earnestly; to beg or demand, as from a figure of authority.
Of unsound mind; insane; demented. [from 17th c.]

examples.english: I'm crazy about you.

current_entries.en_definition: Out of control.
(slang, countable) An insane or eccentric person; a crackpot.
Of food or drink, having the rich taste or thick, smooth texture of cream, whether or not it actually contains cream.
Having the colour of cream.
Something created such as an invention or artwork.
The act of creating something.
All which exists.
Tending to create things, or having the ability to create; often, excellently, in a novel fashion, or any or all of these.
(of a created thing) Original, expressive and imaginative.
Province of Lombardy, Italy.
City and capital of Cremona.
A crucial or decisive point or situation; a turning point.

examples.english: In a crisis, you must get in touch with your teacher.

current_entries.en_definition: An unstable situation, in political, social, economic or military affairs, especially one involving an impending abrupt change.
(medicine) A sudden change in the course of a disease, usually at which point the patient is expected to either recover or die.
(psychology) A traumatic or stressful change in a person's life.
(drama) A point in a drama at which a conflict reaches a peak before being resolved.
(thermodynamics) The temperature and pressure at which the vapour density of the gas and liquid phases of a fluid are equal, at which point there is no difference between gas and liquid.
(mathematics) A point at which the derivative of a function is zero or undefined, such as a minimum, a maximum, a point of inflection or, in multivariate calculus, a saddle point.
(idiomatic) A tear shed insincerely, in a false display of sorrow or some other emotion.
(idiomatic, in the plural) A display of tears that is forced or false.
(chemistry) A cup-shaped piece of laboratory equipment used to contain chemical compounds when heating them to very high temperatures.
A heat-resistant container in which metals are melted, usually at temperatures above 500¬ΑC, commonly made of graphite with clay as a binder.
(Christianity, often, capitalized) The death of Jesus Christ on the Cross.
An execution by being nailed or tied to an upright cross and left to hang there until dead.
In a natural, untreated state.
Lacking tact or taste.
Lacking concealing elements.
Any substance in its natural state.
(nautical, in the days of sail) A frigate or other vessel, detached from the fleet, to cruise independently in search of the enemy or its merchant ships.
(military, nautical) A class of fast warships of medium tonnage, having a long cruising range but less armour and firepower than a battleship.
(historical) A fighter or participant in the medieval Crusades.
(figurative) A person engaged in a crusade.
(informal, by extension) The human object of such infatuation or affection.
To press between two hard objects; to squeeze so as to alter the natural shape or integrity, or to force together into a mass.
To oppress or grievously burden.
To overcome completely; to subdue totally.
(intransitive) To be or become broken down or in, or pressed into a smaller volume or area, by external weight or force.
The science and technology of the production of very low temperatures.
The scientific study of low-temperature phenomena.
A solid composed of an array of atoms or molecules possessing long-range order and arranged in a pattern which is periodic in three dimensions.
A piece of glimmering, shining mineral resembling ice or glass.
A fine type of glassware, or the material used to make it.
The glass over the dial of a watch case.
The act or process by which a substance in solidifying assumes the form and structure of a crystal, or becomes crystallized.
The body formed by crystallizing.
The formation of a solid from a solution, melt, vapour or from a different solid phase
(religion) The veneration, devotion, and religious rites given to a deity (especially in a historical polytheistic context), or (in a Christian context) to a saint; a subset of worship.

(chiefly, derogatory) A group, sect or movement following an unorthodox religious or philosophical system of beliefs, especially one in which members remove and exclude themselves from greater society, including family members not part of the cult, and show extreme devotion to a charismatic leader.
Learned in the ways of civilized society; civilized; refined.
Artificially developed.
The flowering plant Cuminum cyminum, in the family Apiaceae.
Its aromatic long seed, used as a spice, notably in Indian, Middle Eastern, and Mexican cookery.
A city and comune, the capital of the province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy.
A province of Piedmont, in northwestern Italy.
Any regulation requiring people to be off the streets and in their homes by a certain time.
A signal indicating this time.
Inquisitiveness; the tendency to ask and learn about things by asking questions, investigating, or exploring. [from 17th c.]

examples.english: Curiosity killed the cat.

current_entries.en_definition: A unique or extraordinary object which arouses interest. [from 17th c.]
The shape of something curved.
(mathematics) The extent to which a subspace is curved within a metric space.
The legal right to take care of something or somebody, especially children.

examples.english: The man was held in police custody.

current_entries.en_definition: Temporary possession or care of somebody else's property.
(Roman Catholicism) An area under the jurisdiction of a custos within the Order of Friars Minor.
The state of being imprisoned or detained, usually pending a trial.

Possessing physical features, behaviors, personality traits or other properties that are mainly attributed to infants and small or cuddly animals; e.g. fair, dainty, round, and soft physical features, disproportionately large eyes and head, playfulness, fragility, helplessness, curiosity or shyness, innocence, affectionate behavior.

examples.english: This baby penguin is too cute!

current_entries.en_definition: Lovable, charming, attractive or pleasing, especially in a youthful, dainty, quaint or fun-spirited way.
A thin slice of meat, usually fried.
A chop, a specific piece of meat (especially pork, chicken, or beef) cut from the side of an animal.

(science fiction, uncountable) A subgenre of science fiction which focuses on computer or information technology and virtual reality juxtaposed with a degree of breakdown or radical change in the social order.
(music genre, uncountable) A musical genre related to the punk movement that makes use of electronic sounds such as synthesizers.
A world of information accessed through the Internet.

examples.english: No one says "cyberspace" anymore.

current_entries.en_definition: (science fiction) A three-dimensional representation of virtual space in a computer network.
A complete rotation of anything.
A process that returns to its beginning and then repeats itself in the same sequence.
A programme on a washing machine, dishwasher, or other such device.
To go through a cycle or to put through a cycle.
(uncountable, organic compound) A simple alicyclic hydrocarbon, C4H8; a light inflammable gas.
(countable, organic chemistry) Any derivative of this compound.
(informal) A father, a male parent.

examples.english: Mum, why doesn't Dad clean his own study?

current_entries.en_definition: Used to address one's father; often capitalized.
(historical) A former kingdom in West Africa, existing from c. 1600βÄ™1904 and located in the southern part of present-day Benin.
(historical) Former name of Benin: a country in West Africa; used from 1958βÄ™1975. Official name: Republic of Dahomey.
A wild flowering plant of species Bellis perennis of the family Asteraceae, with a yellow head and white petals

examples.english: A little nap and, just like that, I'm as fresh as a daisy.

current_entries.en_definition: Any of numerous other flowering plants of various species, mostly among the asterids.
